label: Five-row baseline
hypothesis: Existing retrieval before source-aware acquisition establishes the comparison floor.
notes: Baseline over the first five benchmark rows used during acquisition debugging.

label: First acquisition with narrow clinical linking
hypothesis: Public source slices should improve targeted gaps, but the clinical label index may be too narrow for drugs, genes, and oncology terms.
acquisition_scope: DailyMed top drugs; NCI pages; MedlinePlus Genetics genes; six PubMed topics linked with clinical label index
notes: Rejected as a learned negative control: it reduced recall@10 because the narrower linker missed key source anchors.

label: Broader biomedical linking
hypothesis: The broader biomedical label index should recover drug, gene, disease, lab, and oncology anchors missed by the first pass.
acquisition_scope: Same source families rebuilt or relinked with build/umls_biomedicine_search_label_index.sqlite
notes: Accepted: improved five-row verdicts to all good and recovered all expected concepts by rank 20.

label: Expanded association topics
hypothesis: Expanding from six PubMed topics to the full measured 25-topic queue should add useful association evidence without degrading the accepted round.
acquisition_scope: Full live-bundle PubMed association topics and remaining DailyMed drugs
notes: Neutral on the focused rows; kept the accepted quality level but did not improve recall.

label: Targeted severe-hypoglycemia and corrected DailyMed
hypothesis: Focused evidence for the remaining miss plus correctly anchored clopidogrel/osimertinib DailyMed labels should preserve the accepted round.
acquisition_scope: Severe-hypoglycemia PubMed slice and corrected DailyMed clopidogrel/osimertinib source subset
notes: Neutral on the focused rows; severe hypoglycemia remains a rank-11 crowding issue, not an evidence absence issue.

label: Six-row baseline
hypothesis: The source-acquisition benchmark should include all rows that produced acquisition signal, not only the initial focused five rows.
notes: Baseline over paragraphs 156-161 before staged acquired evidence.

label: Accepted fixed-linker acquisition
hypothesis: The accepted source set plus default-ambiguity linker fix should improve the full acquisition benchmark without adding false positives.
acquisition_scope: Accepted broader biomedical artifacts, expanded PubMed topics, targeted severe-hypoglycemia slice, and default-ambiguity fixed DailyMed clopidogrel/osimertinib build
notes: Accepted: improved six-row verdicts from 5 good / 1 mixed to 6 good / 0 mixed and recall@20 from 34/35 to 35/35 with no disallowed@10 increase.

label: Missing high-citation OpenAlex candidate
hypothesis: Recent high-citation articles missing from existing OpenAlex and recent PubMed corpora should preserve the accepted source-acquisition benchmark before any broad promotion.
acquisition_scope: OpenAlex five-year highly cited article sweep from measured clinical/drug/lab/condition query file, excluding current OpenAlex and recent PubMed corpora
notes: Neutral on the accepted six-row acquisition benchmark; kept as candidate evidence because the full default benchmark showed slight crowding before source-aware routing/filtering.

label: Current default 161-row benchmark
hypothesis: Before broad promotion, the default server corpus should establish a same-query baseline over the current 161-row paragraph benchmark.
acquisition_scope: Current default local server vector/doc set without the new missing high-citation shard
notes: Default baseline: 144 good / 17 mixed, found@10 831/835, disallowed@10 0/161.

label: Missing high-citation OpenAlex default-path test
hypothesis: Broadly adding recent high-citation missing articles should not reduce default benchmark quality or crowd expected concepts.
acquisition_scope: Current default local server vector/doc set plus the missing high-citation OpenAlex candidate shard
notes: Rejected for default promotion in this form: full benchmark changed to 143 good / 18 mixed and found@10 830/835, with no disallowed@10 increase. Keep as candidate evidence pending filtering or source-aware routing.
